sn21.1:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn21.1:1.3: There Ven. Mahā Moggallāna addressed the monks,
sn21.1:1.5: “Yes, friend,” the monks responded to him.
sn21.1:2.2: “Friends, once as I was withdrawn in seclusion, this train of thought arose to my awareness,
sn21.1:2.3: ‘“Noble silence, noble silence,” it is said.
sn21.1:2.5: Then the thought occurred to me,
sn21.1:2.6: ‘There is the case where a monk, with the stilling of directed thoughts & evaluations,1 enters & remains in the second jhāna: rapture & pleasure born of concentration, unification of awareness free from directed thought & evaluation—internal assurance.
sn21.1:2.8: So, with the stilling of directed thoughts & evaluations, I entered & remained in the second jhāna: rapture & pleasure born of concentration, unification of awareness free from directed thought & evaluation—internal assurance.
sn21.1:2.9: While I remained in that (mental) dwelling, I was assailed by attention to perceptions dealing with directed thought.2
sn21.1:3.1: “Then the Blessed One, coming to me through his (psychic) power, said,
sn21.1:3.2: ‘Moggallāna. Moggallāna. Brahman, don’t be heedless of noble silence. Establish your mind in noble silence. Make your mind unified in noble silence.
sn21.1:3.3: Concentrate your mind in noble silence.’ So at a later time, with the stilling of directed thoughts & evaluations, I entered & remained in the second jhāna: rapture & pleasure born of concentration, unification of awareness free from directed thought & evaluation—internal assurance.
sn21.1:3.4: “When one, speaking rightly, would say of someone,
sn21.1:3.5: ‘A disciple attained to greatness of direct knowledge through the assistance of the Teacher,’ it’s of me that one speaking rightly would say,
sn21.1:3.6: ‘A disciple attained to greatness of direct knowledge through the assistance of the Teacher.’”
